title: Every hook logs its invocations; encode as a CODE_STANDARDS rule
description: Hook firings are invisible today — only gate-watch leaves a heartbeat, so invocation frequency can't be observed (Brooks). Two parts: (1) architect extends CODE_STANDARDS.md ## Hooks with a checkable rule: every hook script appends an invocation record {ts, script, event, outcome} via the shared hook-lib helper to a single .excn hook-invocations progress file (*_progress.json class) before exiting, including no-op/disabled exits, never breaking the fail-safe contract; (2) builder implements the hook-lib helper, retrofits all stamped hook scripts, and doctor's firing/stale heartbeat reads the unified log. Standard change is chartered: alignment gate + Team Lead approval.
acceptance_criteria: CODE_STANDARDS.md carries the checkable hook-logging rule (alignment PASS)
All stamped hook scripts append invocation records via hook-lib, including disabled/no-op paths
doctor heartbeats read the unified invocation log
Fail-safe contract intact: logging failure never blocks or surfaces
fix: CODE_STANDARDS ## Hooks invocation-logging rule (alignment PASS) + hook-lib logInvocation (self-catching, rolling 5000) + every wired hook logs each exit path + doctor heartbeats read the unified hook-invocations_progress.json. code-standards PASS first-run, package-qa PASS.
